name: Tapsilog
steps: Marinate beef tapa in soy sauce, vinegar, and garlic overnight.
Pan-fry the beef tapa until cooked through.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic and mixing with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve beef tapa, rice, and egg on a plate with vinegar dipping sauce.

name: Chicken Adobo
steps: Combine chicken, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, and pepper in a pot.
Marinate for 30 minutes (optional, for deeper flavor).
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 30-35 minutes until chicken is tender.
Optional: Pan-fry chicken to brown before serving.
Serve over steamed rice with the adobo sauce.

name: Sinigang na Baboy
steps: Boil pork ribs in water for 30 minutes until tender.
Add tomatoes, onion, and tamarind soup base.
Add radish and string beans, simmer 5 minutes.
Add eggplant and kangkong, cook 2 more minutes.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Lumpiang Shanghai
steps: Mix ground pork, carrot, onion, and garlic in a bowl.
Place a spoonful of filling on a lumpia wrapper and roll tightly.
Seal the edge with beaten egg.
Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy.
Serve with sweet chili sauce or banana ketchup.

name: Pancit Canton
steps: Soak noodles in warm water until soft.
Stir-fry garlic and onion in oil.
Add chicken and cook until done.
Add vegetables and stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add noodles and soy sauce, toss well. Serve hot.

name: Lechon Kawali
steps: Boil pork belly with salt, peppercorns, bay leaves, and garlic for 45 minutes.
Drain and let dry, then score the skin.
Deep fry until skin is golden and crispy.
Drain on paper towels.
Chop and serve with liver sauce or vinegar.

name: Kare-Kare
steps: Boil oxtail for 2 hours until tender.
Sauté garlic and onion, add peanut butter and some broth.
Add vegetables and simmer until cooked.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with bagoong on the side.

name: Halo-Halo
steps: Add sweet beans, nata de coco, kaong, and ube halaya to a glass.
Top with shaved ice.
Pour evaporated milk over the ice.
Add leche flan and ube ice cream on top.
Serve immediately with a long spoon.

name: Chicken Arroz Caldo
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Add rice and water, bring to a boil.
Simmer until rice is porridge-like, about 30 minutes.
Serve topped with green onions, hard-boiled eggs, and a squeeze of calamansi.

name: Pork Sisig
steps: Boil pork belly for 30 minutes until tender.
Grill or pan-fry until charred on all sides.
Chop finely and mix with onion, garlic, chili, calamansi, and mayonnaise.
Top with raw eggs.
Serve sizzling on a hot plate.

name: Pandesal
steps: Mix flour, yeast, sugar, and salt in a bowl.
Add melted butter and warm milk, knead until smooth.
Let rise for 1 hour until doubled.
Shape into rolls, coat with bread crumbs.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes until golden.

name: Champorado
steps: Rinse rice and add to pot with water.
Bring to boil, then simmer until rice is porridge-like.
Add cocoa powder, sugar, and salt. Stir well.
Add evaporated milk and simmer 2 more minutes.
Serve hot with dried fish (tuyo) on the side.

name: Tortang Giniling
steps: Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add ground pork and cook until no longer pink.
Beat eggs and mix with cooked pork.
Pour into hot oiled pan.
Fry until golden brown on both sides.

name: Bibingka
steps: Mix rice flour, sugar, and baking powder.
Add coconut milk, eggs, and melted butter. Stir until smooth.
Pour into banana-lined molds.
Top with grated cheese.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 20-25 minutes.

name: Puto
steps: Mix rice flour, sugar, and baking powder.
Add coconut milk, eggs, and melted butter. Stir until smooth.
Pour into puto molds.
Steam for 15-20 minutes.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Turon
steps: Peel bananas and slice in half lengthwise.
Place banana and jackfruit on lumpia wrapper.
Sprinkle with sugar.
Roll tightly and seal with water.
Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.

name: Adobong Manok sa Gata
steps: Combine chicken, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and bay leaves.
Marinate 30 minutes.
Bring to boil, then simmer 20 minutes.
Add coconut milk and chili peppers.
Simmer until sauce thickens, about 10 minutes.

name: Goto
steps: Boil beef tripe for 1 hour until tender.
Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
Add rice and water, bring to boil.
Add tripe and simmer until porridge-like.
Serve topped with hard-boiled egg, calamansi, and fish sauce.

name: Biko
steps: Cook glutinous rice with coconut milk, sugar, and salt.
Stir constantly until rice is very sticky and sweet.
Transfer to a greased pan.
Top with latik if using.
Let cool before cutting into squares.

name: Suman
steps: Soak glutinous rice for 4 hours or overnight.
Mix with coconut milk, sugar, and salt.
Place rice in banana leaves, fold and tie.
Steam for 45 minutes.
Serve with latik or sugar.

name: Pancit Bihon
steps: Soak rice noodles in warm water until soft.
Stir-fry garlic and onion in oil.
Add chicken and cook until done.
Add vegetables and stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add noodles and soy sauce, toss well. Serve hot.

name: Inihaw na Manok
steps: Marinate chicken in soy sauce, calamansi, garlic, sugar, and pepper for 2 hours.
Stuff lemongrass inside the chicken.
Grill over medium heat, turning often.
Cook for 40-50 minutes until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Let rest 5 minutes before chopping. Serve with rice and sawsawan.

name: Inihaw na Bangus
steps: Score the milkfish on both sides.
Rub with salt, pepper, and calamansi juice.
Stuff with tomatoes, onion, and garlic.
Grill over medium heat for 20-25 minutes per side.
Serve with rice and a dipping sauce of soy sauce and calamansi.

name: Daing na Bangus
steps: Marinate fish in vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper for 1 hour.
Pat dry with paper towels.
Pan-fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve with rice and a fried egg.

name: Tortang Talong
steps: Grill eggplants until skin is charred and flesh is soft.
Peel off the skin and mash the flesh.
Beat eggs and mix with mashed eggplant, salt, and pepper.
Pour into hot oiled pan.
Fry until golden brown on both sides.

name: Fried Chicken
steps: Mix flour, cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Coat chicken pieces in the flour mixture.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
Deep fry chicken for 12-15 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Drain on paper towels. Serve with rice and gravy.

name: Pork Adobo
steps: Combine pork, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, and pepper.
Marinate for 30 minutes.
Bring to boil, then simmer for 40-45 minutes until pork is tender.
Optional: Pan-fry to brown before serving.
Serve over steamed rice.

name: Sinigang na Hipon
steps: Boil water with tomatoes, onion, and tamarind soup base.
Add radish and string beans, simmer 5 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook 3-5 minutes until pink.
Add eggplant and kangkong, cook 2 more minutes.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Nilagang Baka
steps: Boil beef shank for 1.5 hours until tender.
Add tomatoes, onion, and fish sauce.
Add potatoes and carrots, simmer 10 minutes.
Add cabbage and string beans, cook 5 more minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

name: Bulalo
steps: Boil beef shank with onion and peppercorns for 2 hours.
Skim off any scum that rises to the top.
Add corn and potatoes, simmer 15 minutes.
Add cabbage and carrots, cook 10 more minutes.
Season with fish sauce. Serve with rice.

name: Tinola
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Add water and bring to boil.
Add green papaya and simmer 15 minutes.
Add spinach and fish sauce. Serve hot.

name: Paksiw na Lechon
steps: Place lechon in a pot.
Add vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, bay leaves, peppercorns, and sugar.
Bring to boil without stirring.
Simmer for 30 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve with rice.

name: Paksiw na Isda
steps: Place fish in a pot.
Add vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, bay leaves, and peppercorns.
Bring to boil without stirring.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until fish is cooked.
Serve with rice.

name: Bicol Express
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
Add pork and cook until lightly browned.
Add coconut milk and bagoong, simmer 20 minutes.
Add chili peppers, cook 5 more minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

name: Laing
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and pork in oil.
Add coconut milk and bagoong, bring to simmer.
Add taro leaves and chili peppers.
Simmer for 30 minutes until leaves are tender.
Serve with rice.

name: Caldereta
steps: Boil beef for 1 hour until tender.
Sauté garlic and onion, add tomato sauce.
Add beef, potatoes, and carrots. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add bell pepper, liver spread, and olives.
Simmer 5 more minutes. Serve with rice.

name: Mechado
steps: Marinate beef in soy sauce for 30 minutes.
Boil beef for 1 hour until tender.
Sauté garlic and onion, add tomato sauce.
Add beef, potatoes, and carrots. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add bell pepper. Serve with rice.

name: Menudo
steps: Boil pork for 30 minutes until tender.
Sauté garlic and onion, add tomato sauce.
Add pork, liver, potatoes, and carrots. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add bell pepper and raisins.
Simmer 5 more minutes. Serve with rice.

name: Afritada
steps: Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Add tomato sauce, sugar, and water.
Add potatoes and carrots, simmer 20 minutes.
Add bell pepper. Serve with rice.

name: Pinakbet
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
Add shrimp paste and stir.
Add squash and a little water, simmer 5 minutes.
Add eggplant, string beans, and okra.
Simmer until vegetables are tender. Serve with rice.

name: Dinengdeng
steps: Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
Add shrimp paste and stir.
Add squash and water, simmer 5 minutes.
Add remaining vegetables.
Simmer until tender. Serve with rice.

name: Ginisang Monggo
steps: Boil mung beans until soft, about 30 minutes.
Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
Add pork if using, cook until done.
Add boiled mung beans and water.
Add spinach and fish sauce. Serve with rice.

name: Lumpiang Sariwa
steps: Sauté garlic, then add tofu and vegetables.
Season with soy sauce, cook 5 minutes.
Place filling on lumpia wrapper, roll tightly.
Serve fresh (not fried) with peanut sauce.
Garnish with lettuce and carrots.

name: Pancit Malabon
steps: Soak noodles in warm water until soft.
Sauté garlic, onion, pork, shrimp, and squid.
Add achuete oil, coconut milk, and shrimp paste.
Add noodles and toss well.
Top with sliced hard-boiled eggs and chicharon.

name: Adobong Puti
steps: Combine pork, vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, peppercorns, and water.
Bring to boil, then simmer 40 minutes until pork is tender.
Optional: Pan-fry to brown before serving.
Serve over steamed rice.

name: Adobong Pusit
steps: Combine squid, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, onion, bay leaves, and peppercorns.
Marinate 15 minutes.
Bring to boil, then simmer 10 minutes.
Do not overcook or squid will be tough.
Serve with rice.

name: Crispy Pata
steps: Boil pork leg with salt, garlic, bay leaves, and peppercorns for 1.5 hours.
Drain and let dry completely.
Score the skin in a crisscross pattern.
Deep fry until skin is golden and crispy.
Drain and chop. Serve with liver sauce or vinegar.

name: Chicharon Bulaklak
steps: Clean pork ruffles thoroughly.
Boil with vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper for 20 minutes.
Drain and let dry.
Deep fry until crispy and puffed.
Serve with spicy vinegar dipping sauce.

name: Relyenong Bangus
steps: Boil milkfish for 10 minutes, then carefully debone.
Sauté garlic, onion, carrot, and ground pork.
Mix in egg, salt, and pepper.
Stuff the fish with the mixture.
Steam for 20 minutes, then slice and serve.

name: Embutido
steps: Mix ground pork, bread crumbs, eggs, carrot, onion, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Add raisins and pickle relish.
Shape into a log on aluminum foil.
Steam for 45 minutes.
Let cool before slicing. Serve with rice.

name: Morcon
steps: Lay beef flat, layer with hotdogs, pickles, carrot, bell pepper, raisins, and egg slices.
Roll tightly and tie with kitchen string.
Sear in oil until browned on all sides.
Add tomato sauce and water, simmer 1 hour.
Slice and serve with rice.

name: Fish Kinilaw
steps: Combine fish with calamansi juice, marinate 10 minutes.
Add cucumber, onion, ginger, and chili.
Season with salt.
Toss gently.
Serve immediately as an appetizer or pulutan.

name: Dinuguan
steps: Sauté garlic and onion.
Add pork and cook until browned.
Add vinegar and simmer 10 minutes.
Add pork blood and stir continuously.
Add chili and sugar. Simmer 20 minutes. Serve with rice.

name: Batchoy
steps: Boil pork bones for 1 hour to make broth.
Sauté garlic and onion, add pork liver and intestines.
Add broth and bring to boil.
Add noodles and cook 3 minutes.
Serve topped with hard-boiled egg and crushed chicharon.

name: Pares
steps: Boil beef for 1 hour until tender.
Sauté garlic and onion, add soy sauce and sugar.
Add beef and cornstarch slurry.
Simmer until sauce thickens.
Serve with rice and a side of beef broth soup.

name: Beef Kansi
steps: Boil beef shank and bone marrow for 2 hours.
Add onion, garlic, and peppercorns.
Season with fish sauce and calamansi juice.
Simmer until broth is rich and flavorful.
Serve with rice and extra calamansi on the side.

name: Maruya
steps: Mash bananas in a bowl.
Mix in flour, sugar, milk, and egg.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil.
Fry until golden brown on both sides.
Drain on paper towels. Serve with sugar sprinkled on top.

name: Camote Q
steps: Peel and slice sweet potatoes into 1-inch pieces.
Boil in water with brown sugar until syrupy.
Drain and let cool slightly.
Skewer on bamboo sticks.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Banana Cue
steps: Peel bananas and skewer on bamboo sticks.
Heat oil in a pan.
Roll bananas in brown sugar.
Deep fry until sugar caramelizes and bananas are golden.
Drain on paper towels. Serve warm.

name: Ginataang Bilo-Bilo
steps: Cook glutinous rice balls in boiling water until they float.
Add coconut milk, sugar, taro, and sweet potato.
Simmer until vegetables are tender.
Add saba banana and tapioca pearls.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Ginataang Halo-Halo
steps: Combine coconut milk and sugar in a pot.
Add sweet beans, nata de coco, and kaong.
Add saba banana, taro, and tapioca pearls.
Simmer until all ingredients are cooked.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Leche Flan
steps: Caramelize sugar in a llanera (mold) until golden.
Beat eggs, egg yolks, evaporated milk, condensed milk, and vanilla.
Strain the mixture into the caramelized mold.
Steam for 30-35 minutes until set.
Let cool, then refrigerate. Serve chilled.

name: Ube Halaya
steps: Boil ube until soft, then mash thoroughly.
Combine ube with condensed milk, evaporated milk, butter, sugar, and coconut milk.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly.
Cook for 30 minutes until thick and smooth.
Serve warm or chilled. Top with latik if desired.

name: Puto Bumbong
steps: Soak purple rice for 4 hours or overnight.
Mix with coconut milk, sugar, and salt.
Place in bamboo tubes (bumbong).
Steam for 45 minutes.
Serve topped with grated coconut and brown sugar.

name: Sapin-Sapin
steps: Mix glutinous rice flour, coconut milk, sugar, and butter.
Divide into two portions. Mix one portion with ube halaya.
Layer white and purple mixtures in a greased pan.
Steam for 30 minutes.
Top with latik before serving.

name: Tosilog
steps: Fry tocino until caramelized and cooked.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve tocino, rice, and egg together.
Add vinegar on the side for dipping.

name: Longsilog
steps: Fry longganisa until cooked through and slightly crispy.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve longganisa, rice, and egg together.
Add vinegar on the side for dipping.

name: Dangsilog
steps: Fry daing na bangus until crispy.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve bangus, rice, and egg together.
Add vinegar on the side for dipping.

name: Spamsilog
steps: Fry Spam slices until golden and crispy.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve Spam, rice, and egg together.
Add vinegar on the side for dipping.

name: Cornedsilog
steps: Sauté corned beef with garlic until heated through.
Make garlic fried rice by sautéing garlic with day-old rice.
Fry the egg sunny-side up.
Serve corned beef, rice, and egg together.
Add vinegar on the side for dipping.

name: Pan de Coco
steps: Mix flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add coconut milk and melted butter, knead until smooth.
Let rise for 1 hour.
Shape into rolls with coconut filling inside.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes.

name: Ensaymada
steps: Mix flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add butter, eggs, and milk, knead until smooth.
Let rise for 1 hour.
Shape into rolls, brush with butter.
Top with grated cheese and sugar. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 15 minutes.

name: Sinigang na Bangus
steps: Boil water with tomatoes, onion, and tamarind soup base.
Add radish and string beans, simmer 5 minutes.
Add milkfish and cook 10 minutes.
Add eggplant and kangkong, cook 2 more minutes.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Kinilaw na Tanigue
steps: Combine fish with calamansi juice, marinate 10 minutes.
Add cucumber, onion, ginger, and chili.
Season with salt.
Toss gently.
Serve immediately as an appetizer or pulutan.
